text:
Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight - All Chapter 1 Red Bricks and Mission Collectibles
In this Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight walkthrough, we show you exactly where to find every collectible in Chapter 1 missions: Infiltration, New Beginnings, Carmine Falcone, and The Red Hood Gang. Uncover every Bonsai Tree, Surfboard, Disco Ball, Ace Card, Waynetech Cache, and Red Brick locations for Ninja, Nautical, Filthy Rich, and Toxic Waste.

Overwatch - Official 10th Anniversary Theme Behind-the-Scenes Trailer
A decade of Overwatch deserves its own soundtrack. Get a behind-the-scenes look at the making of the Overwatch 10th Anniversary theme, recorded live with a full orchestra. Hear it in-game during the 2026 Anniversary Event and across everything for the future of Overwatch.

#ign #gaming

author: IGN YouTube
text:
Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight - How to Unlock the Absolute Batman Suit
If you have been following Gotham’s latest comic adventures, then you know that Scott Snyder's Absolute Batman has been an incredibly successful, brick-house take on the Caped Crusader. It’s no shocking surprise that TT Games' LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight would pay tribute to the massive new look by including a special unlockable Absolute Batman costume. While he might be a whole lot of Batman to fit into such a small minifigure, this guide will show you exactly how to complete the Chapter 2 "On the Prowl" side activities, track down the escaped zoo animals around Gotham City, and add this absolute unit of a suit to your inventory.

Looking for more LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight gameplay guides? This walkthrough breaks down the easiest methods to secure your new costume early in Chapter 2 or by utilizing Batgirl's radio towers in Chapter 4. Learn how to optimize your Detective Vision mechanics, sneak up on escaped zoo animals, and map out your path through Gotham City efficiently. Stay tuned for more LEGO Batman guides, including how to find Red Bricks, unlock iconic cinematic costumes like Batman '89, and defeat the classic rogues' gallery.

#ign #gaming

author: IGN YouTube
text:
Lego Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight: Iceberg Lounge Vault Puzzle Solution
Early into your playthrough of LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight, your mission to take down Carmine Falcone leads you straight into Gotham City's infamous Iceberg Lounge. While navigating the Penguin's territory, you'll stumble upon a locked vault hidden away in a back room. Cracking the code isn't just for show—inside lies a highly coveted Red Brick that unlocks a sleek cosmetic modifier for the Dark Knight's arsenal. If you're struggling to track down the clues or wrestle with the tricky safe dial controls, this quick puzzle guide has you covered. 

Our step-by-step walkthrough details exactly how to solve the Iceberg Lounge vault puzzle to claim your reward. We break down the locations of both hidden clues scattered across the bar and lounge area, reveal the final three-digit safe combination, and explain how to properly manipulate the analog stick inputs to unlock the door. Grabbing this collectible rewards you with the "Filthy Rich" suit and Batmobile color palette, a unique green-and-black cosmetic inspired by a fusion of Batman and the Riddler. For more gameplay tips, puzzle solutions, and full quest walkthroughs, keep it locked right here.

Forza Horizon 6 is available now for Xbox Series X|S, Xbox Cloud, and PC (Steam and Xbox on PC), with a release on PlayStation 5 (PS5) coming later in 2026.
The legendary "Sound of the Wind" storms into the Horizon Festival. Unlock the 2008 Mazda Furai for 60 PTS on the Festival Playlist.

author: IGN YouTube
text:
The Complete History of Batman Games - Best, Worst, Weirdest
Batman was created more than 80 years ago and has starred in video games for 40 of them, nearly half of his existence, but the dozens of Batman games across countless platforms and console generations have rarely strayed far from the basics. No matter what decade it is, no matter what hardware you’re playing on, odds are that in any given Batman title you’ll be gliding off gargoyles, firing grappling hooks, and flinging Batarangs at guys in straightjackets. 

Batman’s constant reinventions in the comics have left the games mostly playing catch-up for the better part of their history, largely tied to popular movies and TV shows rather than taking the character someplace truly new, but within these constraints, and eventually beyond them, we’ve seen genuine masterpieces with the Batman Arkham series, slept-on gems, unmitigated disasters, and entries so bizarre and inexplicable they belong inside the Black Casebook. LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight is the latest and most comprehensive Bat game to date, which means it’s the perfect time to take stock of what that legacy actually looks like. 

These are the best, worst, and weirdest Batman video games.

00:00 - Intro
01:42 - Batman: Year One (1986 - 1989)
06:58 - The Sunsoft Rises (1989 - 1991)
12:01 - Pure Dynamite (1991)
15:08 - Many Happy Returns (1992)
21:05 - Almost Got ‘Im (1993 - 1994)
26:36 - The Ice Age (1995 - 2003)
31:49 - No Man’s Land (2000 - 2003)
38:38 - The Dawn is Coming (2005 - 2008)
41:59 - Arkham and Everything After (2009 - 2015)
49:36 - Part 10: Batman R.I.P. (2015 - Now)

author: IGN YouTube
text:
Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 – The First Preview | IGN First
With a fourth player added, a new completely open-ended Specialist class, new special Xenomorph types, and a totally new campaign, Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 may not be reinventing the PvE Alien-game wheel, but it doesn’t need to. It’s adding seemingly everything we’d reasonably want in order to take the already well-executed concept from the first Fireteam Elite to the next level. Previewed by Ryan McCaffrey

author: IGN YouTube
text:
Warhammer 40K: Boltgun 2 Hands-on Preview
I've only played a couple hours of Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun 2, blasting through the 45-minute demo as both playable characters, but I've had a rousing time with it. Both characters feel different and exciting with punchy and satisfying weapons. The new level designs – particularly the icy one – have me intrigued to see what else they've cooked up. It's still very much what I'd expect when you say "Warhammer 40K" and "boomer shooter." But that remains a glorious combination. Previewed by Leana Hafer

author: IGN YouTube
text:
Canyons: Official Enemy Hordes Trailer
Hordes aren't a rare inconvenience in Canyons; they're the natural state of things. The Awakened pour in from everywhere: out of the sand, out of the ruins, down from the cliffs. And they almost never come alone — dozens at a time, sometimes hundreds.

There's never a shortage of reasons to fight either. A chance encounter on the road, a raid on your position, clearing a point of interest, holding off a fanatic siege — the canyon always has something new lined up. Enemy density scales with your squad size too, whether you're running solo or with a full crew of four Crusaders. 

Fighting that many enemies at once changes the logic of a gunfight entirely. One well-placed shot won't cut it anymore, and what matters now is position, tempo, and who's actually pulling their weight: who's holding the flank, who's covering the retreat, who threw that grenade, and who's just standing there looking useful. Every skirmish turns into a spectacle though, with bodies scattering, barrels going up, and structures coming down, until at some point your squad stops feeling like victims of the canyons and starts feeling like the reason the canyons are scared.

What might look hopeless solo is very manageable with a squad of Crusaders, and the game gives you plenty of tools to mix and match. Shotguns and submachine guns for when things get close, rifles and machine guns for keeping your distance, heavy weapons for when you need to make a point to an entire crowd at once. Grenades, mines, and explosive barrels become especially satisfying when enemies are kind enough to bunch together, and at some point they always do.

In fights like these, the Crawler stops being a vehicle and becomes a weapon, crushing, ramming, and hosing down whatever hasn't made it back into the dirt fast enough. A crowd that meets a Crawler tends to stop being a crowd pretty quickly.

The environment brings its own layer of fun too. Collapse a rock face, ignite a fuel tank, topple a structure, or just herd a group toward the canyon's edge and let gravity finish the job. Relics and Blood Pacts round it all out, with special abilities that help you handle even the largest waves.

At the end of the day, the horde in Canyons isn't just a lot of enemies on screen. It's the stage where the full toolkit comes out: weapons, the Crawler, the environment, your crew. Every fight becomes a choice about not just whether you'll make it, but how. Because no loadout in the world makes up for knowing what you're doing. Wishlist Canyons on Steam if you're interested: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2708860/Canyons/

author: IGN YouTube
text:
PS5 + Xbox + Nintendo Summer Show Predictions 2026 - Next-Gen Console Watch
Welcome back to Next-Gen Console Watch! This week, Daemon and Max are joined by Nintendo Voice Chat's Logan Plant and Podcast Unlocked's Miranda Sanchez to give their predictions on the summer's big gaming showcases. Both Xbox and PlayStation have showcases in June, and Nintendo can't be too far behind with a new Nintendo Direct. So what do we think will be there? Some real Wolverine gameplay? A closer look at Halo: Campaign Evolved or Gears of War: E-Day? Or maybe Nintendo will make our dreams come true and confirm the Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake. Plus we have last episode's poll and a new poll for you to vote on at IGN.com and Spotify.
